metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5207
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1b, 3e: immunofluorescence imaging of hippocampal neurons stained for endogenous Dock180 (DOCK1) and PSD95 (DLG4) showed partial co-localization. Stainings of Dock180 were apposed to presynaptic synapsin-1 (SYN1). Overexpressed FLAG-Dock180 localized to dendrites and spines.

Figure 4: ELMO and Dock180 (DOCK1) form a complex in hippocampal neurons and function through activating Rac1. Overexpressed Dock180 increased spinogenesis.

Figure 5-7: RhoG is required for spine formation, its activation leads to increased spine head size and functions through the ELMO1/Dock180/Rac pathway.. Experimental description: M&M: "Neuronal Culture and Transfection - Hippocampal neuron cultures were prepared from embryonic day 19 rat embryos as described previously"

antibody:
rabbit polyclonal anti-Dock180 (1:1000, provided by Dr. Matsuda)
goat polyclonal anti-Dock180 antibodies (1:100, N19 and C19, Santa Cruz Biotechnology)
rabbit polyclonal anti-ELMO1 (1:1000, provided by Dr. Ravichandran)
goat polyclonal anti-ELMO1 (1:2000, Abcam)

plasmids:
"FLAG-Dock180, Dock180-ISP, RhoG, and ELMO constructs were kindly provided by Kodi Ravichandran and have been described previously (24, 25, 33)." PMID:12134158 which ultimately leads to PMID:8657152 --> Nucleotide BLAST confirms the human gene DOCK1 was targeted. BLAST of the protein sequence in PMID:8657152 Figure 2 yields uniprot accession Q14185 (human DOCK1).

The uniprot database currently does not contain a record for Rat DOCK1, so instead of annotating the endogenous protein we annotate the overexpressed species (human).

checks.label: OWL Consistency
checks.error_message: No inferences attributed to this model graph in the materializer output. Likely a parse failure or other materializer skip — re-run on this model alone to investigate.
